concept: Ingredient / Food Material Chosen for a Preparation
description: A food material chosen as one part of a dish or drink being prepared. The same onion is ordinary food in a basket and an ingredient once the cook relates it to a particular recipe or meal. 'nuora' (food) covers nourishment generally; 'phareme' identifies the part before and during preparation.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'phare' places the word beside 'pharelo' (cook), close to the work of preparation. Grounded 'me' leaves the ingredient as material in the cook's hand before the dish changes it.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun according to context: 'mia ha phareme kanu.' (I choose this ingredient.) A recipe, dish, amount, or preparation stage may appear around it. The word is culinary; materials in non-food craft remain 'muralo' (material) where that module applies.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Naming an ingredient keeps the route from garden, market, store, or gift available beside the finished dish.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A practiced cook knew which handful, pinch, or seasonal substitute would still let the meal come together.

tags.activity: food selected for use in a preparation
tags.creation: one material from which a dish or drink is made
tags.physical: an edible substance before or during preparation
